title: Intensely Buttery Brazilian Tahini Pilaf
description: A rich and creamy Brazilian dish featuring tahini paste and a soffritto base, perfect for a crowd, this intensely buttery pilaf is a shojin-friendly delight that combines the deep flavors of Brazil with the nutty taste of tahini.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the rice to the saucepan and stir to coat the rice in the oil and mix with the onion and garlic.
Add the vegetable broth to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 18 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
While the rice is cooking, mix the melted butter and tahini paste in a small bowl until well combined.
When the rice is done, fluff it with a fork and stir in the butter and tahini mixture until the rice is well coated.
Season the pilaf with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
